name: Royal Chicken Briyani
recipeInstructions:
- Cook the rice: Rinse 500g basmati rice until water runs clear. Drain well.
- Heat 2 1/2 tablespoons ghee in a large pot over medium heat.
- Add 1 finely chopped onion and fry until golden brown.
- Sprinkle saffron or saffron strands, 5 cardamom pods, 3 whole cloves, 1 inch cinnamon stick, and 1/2 teaspoon ginger powder. Stir to coat the rice with ghee.
- Add rinsed rice, rose water or essence, and salt to chicken stock. Bring mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and steam for 20 minutes without lifting lid or stirring.
- Cook the chicken: In a separate pan, heat half the ghee and fry almonds until golden brown. Remove and set aside.
- Add sultanas and fry for a few seconds, then remove and set aside.
- Add potatoes and cook until browned, then remove and set aside.
- In the same pan, add remaining ghee and sauté onions, garlic, ginger until onions are soft and golden.
- Stir in chili powder, black pepper, turmeric, cumin, salt, tomatoes, yogurt, mint leaves, cardamom, and cinnamon stick. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
- Add the cooked chicken pieces and potatoes, then stir to coat with the spice mixture. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until chicken is tender (about 35-45 minutes). If needed, add a little hot water to prevent sticking.
- Combine: Gently mix the cooked rice and chicken into a serving dish. Garnish with almonds and sultanas before serving.
